title: Plain-language style guide + fixed "information, not advice / only the agency decides / get free help" framing + readability targets
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task writes the plain-language style guide and the fixed not-advice / non-partisan framing block every guide and answer must carry verbatim, with concrete readability targets, so content is consistently understandable and never drifts into advice.
objective: Write the plain-language style guide with readability targets and the fixed verbatim not-advice + non-partisan framing block that every guide and answer must include.
acceptanceCriteria: Encodes reading-level targets (grade <= 6-8; never publish above grade 10) and concrete sentence/word/structure rules
Specifies the fixed not-advice + non-partisan framing block ("information, not legal/benefits advice / only the agency decides / get free help") that every guide and answer must carry verbatim
Reviewed and signed off by a plain-language reviewer and a credentialed benefits expert (recorded in the reviewers ledger)
